_source: Curated from a saved LinkedIn webarchive (2026-06). NOT live-fetched — this file is the contract (content tokens / slugs for copy). Edit here; the site renders from these slugs.
name: Robert DeLanghe
headline: AI agents write the production code. I write the contract it runs against.
intro: I'm Bobby. I chase the parts of code that feel like math, and build the systems that keep AI agents inside the job you gave them.
summary: I make agent-authored code accountable by construction: an agent can only touch what its capabilities allow, with effects bound to a signed owner and checked in CI, so permission and accountability are settled by the same check. Open-sourced as prx; shipped in production at Aura.

experience.what: Capability security for AI agents: software delivered by agents, governed like infrastructure. Generalized from my Aura work into open tooling.
bullets: Build prx, guest-room, and claude-box: an object-capability toolchain that bounds an AI agent to exactly the access it holds.
Tie privileged actions to a signed owner and an auditable in-toto/SLSA provenance chain; the Rust systems layer is door-peercred, git-ast, and hooksmith.

experience.what: On Commerce, the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems. I ship the integration and automation backbone, and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.
bullets: Wrote the orchestration CLI that replaced Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan with one source of truth, compiling into every downstream artifact: schemas, docs, tests, agent context.
Built the validation layer that checks generated outputs against Gherkin specs and golden sets, with provenance tracked and sign-off bound to an owner role in CI, not a person.

experience.what: Owned the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team, spanning static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.
bullets: Shipped static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline, blocking whole defect classes at merge instead of catching them after. Critical bugs fell 20% across the team.

experience.what: Delivered the event-planning platform for a major Brooklyn arts center.
bullets: Derived event status from attached documents, not a human-set flag, across the venue's large concerts.

experience.what: Automated just-in-time production for an AR and print-making studio.
bullets: Eliminated edition-numbering errors with a database that issued serial edition IDs on request, making the invalid state unrepresentable.

experience.what: Engineered the contact-management system for the manufacturing publication.
bullets: Designed the schema that moved spreadsheet data into a relational database, making malformed records unrepresentable.

experience.what: Type theory in Haskell, using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.
bullets: Completed a selective 12-week programming retreat researching Haskell type theory and how a well-constrained type narrows the space of possible implementations.

seeking.label: Open to new roles
seeking.focus: Senior / founding engineering on agent infrastructure: agent safety and code provenance
seeking.detail: Senior engineering on teams building agentic products. I bring direct experience shipping agent-authored systems and the controls that make them trustworthy in production. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.
